name: Leave a Voicemail That Actually Gets Called Back
description: When the agent reaches a target's voicemail on a first or early-stage outreach call, leave a message that produces a callback — short, specific, low-pressure, and ending with a clean reason to call back.
disclaimer: Skills assume the call is solicited by the operator. NEVER use these to power a mass-cold-call campaign without the operator's explicit per-prospect authorisation.

context.when_to_use: Agent has placed an authorised outreach call and the line has gone to the target's voicemail. The operator wants a callback (not just to 'have left a message').
preconditions: Operator has authorised this contact attempt specifically.
Agent has a real, specific reason for the call — not a generic 'wanted to connect'.
Agent has a callback number that will be answered or routed sensibly.
Agent knows whether to leave the operator's name, the agent's name, or both.

principles: Under 30 seconds. Long voicemails get deleted at the 20-second mark — every word past that hurts you.
The hook is in the first sentence. State who the call is about and why it might matter to them, immediately.
Specificity over polish. 'About the Hartwell contract' beats 'about a business opportunity' every time.
Give one clean reason to call back, not a menu. Multiple options = decision fatigue = no callback.
Repeat the callback number twice — once early, once at the end. Recipients listen back to capture digits.
Sound like a human leaving a message for one person, not a script being read at a list.

phrases.opener_referral: Hi [target first name], this is [agent name] calling on behalf of [operator name]. [Referrer name] suggested I reach out about [specific topic].
phrases.opener_prior_touch: Hi [target first name], it's [agent name] from [operator]'s office — following up on [specific prior thing: your reply / your post / our exchange at X].
phrases.opener_cold_warm: Hi [target first name], this is [agent name] calling for [operator name]. We work on [one-sentence what], and [specific reason this person in particular].
phrases.the_ask: Wanted to ask you one quick question — best to do it live for two minutes. My number is [number], again that's [number].
phrases.soft_close: No rush, no pitch — if it's not relevant, I'd rather know so I'm not bothering you. Thanks [name], talk soon.

tactics.name: Open with their name, then yours
tactics.script: Say the target's first name first. People listen harder when they hear their own name at the start.

tactics.name: State the one specific reason in sentence two
tactics.script: Use the most specific anchor you have: a referrer, a prior interaction, a public post they made, a customer they have in common. Generic = delete.

tactics.name: Ask one question, not three
tactics.script: Use the `the_ask` phrase. Curiosity is the engine — give them a clean question to come back to, not a sales summary.

tactics.name: Number twice, slowly
tactics.script: Read the callback number with deliberate pauses between groups. Repeat it. Recipients are often writing while they listen.

tactics.name: Out with a permission-giver
tactics.script: Use `soft_close`. Telling them it's OK to say no makes them more likely to call back. Pressure compresses the funnel.

tactics.name: Leave at most two voicemails per cycle
tactics.when: Deciding whether to leave a voicemail at all on attempt N.
tactics.script: Voicemail #1 is welcomed. Voicemail #2 (3-5 days later, only if no response) is acceptable. Voicemail #3 reads as harassment — switch channel instead.

boundaries: Do NOT leave voicemails on consecutive days — minimum 2-3 business days between attempts.
Do NOT fabricate urgency ('time-sensitive', 'closing today') that isn't real.
Do NOT pretend you've spoken before if you haven't.
Do NOT leave a voicemail that name-drops a referrer without the referrer's permission.
Do NOT ramble. If you find yourself past 30 seconds, hang up and re-record (most voicemail systems allow it — press # or *).
success_signals: Target replies via email referencing the voicemail.
Target's assistant calls back to schedule.
Recorded greeting confirms you reached the right person (not a wrong number).
failure_signals: Two voicemails left, no response, no other channel response in 10 business days.
Voicemail box is full (target is unreachable this way — switch channel).
Number rings to a generic shared mailbox (your message will be lost — try the direct dial or email).
Recorded greeting says 'this is the wrong person' / outdated.

exit_strategy.on_success: When the callback comes in, the agent / operator should be ready with the specific reason cited in the voicemail. Mismatched payoff destroys trust.
exit_strategy.on_failure: After 2 voicemails with no response, retire the phone channel for this prospect. Switch to email or, if relevant, a referred-warm introduction.
follow_ups: Log the voicemail in the operator's CRM/notes with timestamp and exact wording used.
Schedule the next-attempt window (no sooner than 3 business days) if a second voicemail is authorised.

required_user_info: Target's first name (correct pronunciation if unusual)
Operator's name and one-line description of what they do
The single most specific reason for the call
Callback number — and confirmation it will be answered or routed
Referrer's name and explicit permission to use it (if applicable)
